reviews.text: Impossible to reach - does not return phone calls. Dan Beatty was recommended to me by a fellow lawyer. I reached out to Dan with an urgent matter and left a voicemail. Beatty did not call me back for a full week. When he did, he called when I was unable to answer the phone and left a voicemail. I tried to returned his call within 15 minutes, but he didn't answer. 5 days later, he has yet to respond to my response voicemail, either.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Jen Hardee,
I am sorry I was not able to return your call in the time you expected. Please understand I am a solo practicing Attorney. I place a priority on the clients I am presently assisting with their legal issues. I have a limited amount of time and I need to be mindful of not over extending my schedule to the detriment of the needs of my current clients. I receive all messages and email inquiries myself. I do not have a staff to screen my incoming message. This does, at times, result in a delay in responding to new inquiries, depending on my current work load and other schedule demands. While I do try to return messages with in 48 hours, there are times I find I need to set a few hours aside at least once a week to return phone messages and emails. Due to the nature of my practice it is difficult for me to take on urgent matters for new clients.
I have not responded to reviews in the past but, I felt it was important to add some context to your comments.
I do note you were calling for a friend's legal matter not your own, we did ultimately talk. I did contact your friend and I did discuss her legal issue, I hope I was helpful with the information I provided to her.
I wish you and your friend all the best in the future." Dan Beatty

As a business and employment litigation attorney for both plaintiffs and defendants with more than 70 jury trials under my belt,  I know the difference between "litigators" and real trial attorneys.  The former are all too common in our profession, adept only at pushing paper, filing motions, and acting like keyboard warriors.  But when cases fail to settle and trial looms, those kinds of lawyers find themselves staring into an abyss.  They then either fold by unfairly compromising their client's case to avoid exposing their lack of trial experience, or stumble through trial with disastrous consequences.

Real trial lawyers, on the other hand, can take any case to the mat and win.  They don't bluff, because they don't need to.  Not only does this achieve better settlements for their clients, but if the case cannot settle these trial lawyers actually win.  Chris and Kevin Moon are both real trial lawyers, and excellent ones at that.  They are both tenacious, brilliant and powerfully effective.  I know this because I have worked with them both, and have seen their skill and dedication first-hand.  Whenever someone calls me with a personal injury matter, I immediately refer them to Chris and Kevin.  Even more, they are the lawyers I would want to represent me if I was ever injured.

If you are potential client, call them!  If you are a defendant on the other side of a case from them, be afraid.  Be VERY afraid.

There is so much more to this story, and there are so many more small things Jim did that made my previous lawyer seem more like a fraud.

I reached out to Mr. Peter Sterling Stern for an elder law and estate planning consultation regarding my family's needs, including MAPT + gift trust. While I understand that legal services can be costly, I was disappointed by Mr. Stern’s dismissive approach during our conversation.

He cut me off multiple times, stating that he “already knew” the information I was providing and didn’t need the context of my case. Additionally, he made it clear that consultations are only available for a fee of $750 for 1.5 hours, which is significantly higher than most other estate planning attorneys who offer complimentary consultations. He clearly stated that he charges for his time. My question is: what legal professional doesn't? On that token, what legal professional is brash within the first conversation?

As a consumer, I expect not only knowledge but also respect and courtesy from any professional I work with. Unfortunately, Mr. Stern’s unwillingness to engage respectfully and inability to provide even a basic modicum of decency to potential clients made it clear that I will be seeking services elsewhere.

My advice to families looking for a family estate attorney: Shop around. There are so many estate attorneys in the Bay Area who are willing to provide tailored consultations, even free! Do your homework, use your resources, call neighboring attorneys. You have the power to protect your family and your family's hard-earned legacy, hand-built from one generation to the next. The last thing you need is an attorney who can't respect your basic human dignity, even at the first conversation. If time is money to a lawyer, respect to YOU, as the client, is priceless.

In the end, choosing an attorney isn’t just about knowledge—it’s about trust and mutual respect. Mr. Stern failed to demonstrate either, and I would not recommend his services. No consultation needed. You selected yourself out, Mr. Stern.

Success Story: A Decade After Bankruptcy — How Ryan Woods Helped Me Rebuild and Thrive

Ten years ago, I filed for bankruptcy. It was one of the hardest decisions I’ve ever had to make. I didn’t know what life would look like afterward, but I knew I needed help. That’s when I found attorney Ryan Woods, who turned out to be more than just legal support — he became a key figure in setting me on a new path.

Fast forward to today: one of my clients, someone I work with in my current industry, just happened to hire Ryan Woods as well. That coincidence reminded me how far I’ve come, and how instrumental Ryan was in helping me get here.

Ryan didn't just help me navigate the bankruptcy process — he connected me with the right people and gave me the tools and strategies to rebuild. Within a year, my credit score had bounced back to around 730, and it’s stayed strong ever since. Within two and a half years, I was able to buy a home — something I never thought would be possible so soon after bankruptcy.

A lot of people assume bankruptcy is the end, but Ryan helped me see it as a fresh start. Over the last decade, I’ve been able to secure business loans, auto loans, home equity lines, and other key financial tools because of the credit discipline I built early on. Today, I am officially debt-free, and I’m already halfway through the principal on my mortgage.

If you're working with Ryan Woods now or thinking about rebuilding your credit after bankruptcy, here's what I want to tell you: don’t stop at the finish line of the bankruptcy process. That’s just the beginning. Keep going, keep learning, and your success will grow.

The moral of the story? When it comes to your goals — whether it’s buying a car, starting a business, or owning a home — it’s rarely a matter of no. It’s a matter of when. Build good habits. Stick to them. And your financial life will follow.

Thank you, Ryan Woods, for helping me reclaim my future. I encourage others to believe in this journey — it is absolutely possible.

I retained Mr. Wood after my Chapter 13 case was dismissed. This dismissal was unopposed by my previous attorney so the case was dismissed by default. After doing some research on my own (I’m sure lawyers love it when their clients do that,) I called numerous attorneys to ask if they could file a motion to oppose the dismissal. Every single attorney I called (apart from Mr. Wood) told me my only option would be to file a new Chapter 13 case and start a new 5 year process of making payments despite already having made almost 4 years of payments.

When Ryan assessed my case, he told me he would immediately file a motion to vacate the dismissal. He was incredibly thorough and made sure to cover everything, asking me tons of questions about my life and the circumstances surrounding my missed payments and subsequent dismissal of my case. Within a few hours, we had a plan of attack, he told me exactly what I needed to do, and the next day he filed a motion to vacate the dismissal. A couple days later my chapter 13 case was reactivated. This meant that I didn’t waste 4 years of monthly payments and that I could continue on with my plan payments to repay my creditors.

Ryan navigated all of this while setting expectations and not making any promises. I did however have total confidence in his abilities and I knew that he would be my best option if I wanted a fighting chance at getting this resolved. It’s so clear that Ryan deeply cared about me as a client. Ryan gave me back my sanity, my happiness, and allowed me to continue on my pathway to financial freedom. My once low-interest-rate payment on my credit card spiraled out of control; they started charging over 22% interest monthly on my cards. I tried and tried to make all the payments until I realized this is an uphill battle that is getting worse each month. I reached the point that I did not have enough money monthly to pay them now. I called my creditors asked them if they could lower the interest rate back to the 6.99% or lower than what it is currently; explained that I was struggling to make the payments at this time; all I heard was No we cannot do that. I must say, I was so worried about the thought of filing bankruptcy. I was scared and I felt bad to have to do this to my creditors but they were not willing to work with me. I then decided to research bankruptcy attorneys. I called a few many had a recording asking me to leave a message, during my search online I came across The Law Offices of Lin & Wood they have an office in Redwood City and Fremont. I live in Hayward so the Fremont office worked for me.  I then went on the Better Business Bureau website to see if they were an Accredited Business and if they had any complaints filed against them? I also wanted to see the rating giving to them by the Better Business Bureau. I was thrilled to see they were Accredited and holds an A+ rating; I felt good knowing this information. I guess my biggest concern was if I was going to file I needed to feel comfortable with the law firm that would walk me through this huge endeavor I was about to undertake financially.  I was confident with their BBB  A+rating I located the best firm  in the east bay.  My first attempt calling the office Mr. Ryan Wood answered the phone; I explained my situation and the urgency to get this started.  He listened without interrupting me, when I stopped he started providing me with the needed information.  He also squeezed me into his schedule the same day I was truly impressed.  I met with him he explained everything in a way that was easy for me to understand what was going to happen step by step. I retained him that day; he instructed me to gather the latest invoice for all my credit cards as well as any other debt I am obligated to pay then turn it into him. He informed me I had to fill out a monthly expense sheet that he would provide; this expense sheet assists him in determining what type of bankruptcy would be best for me to file chapter 13 or 7. I have to admit Mr. Ryan wood told me the truth; each step happened just like he said they would. I am pleased to say I now live comfortably without worrying how am I going to pay my bills any longer and I do not have any creditors sending me letters or calling my phone or work. He does allow you some time I believe it was 60 days to pay the full down; he explained the case would be on hold not being worked until they received the full down payment.  I decided I needed to pay this now to get the ball rolling even if I had to borrow it; I didn't want any wage garnishments to start? I did pay it in full within a day; three months later I met him at the courthouse in Oakland; shortly following the court appearance my bankruptcy case was done.  Thanks to Mr. Ryan Wood I am now living a stress-free life.  He is truly the best and has a vast amount of experience in bankruptcy law!!  You're in good hands if you retain him.  I hope this review helps calm your nerves; I know how nervous I was at the beginning stages. Good luck!

I recently had the opportunity to work with The Law Office of Lauren Rios on a probate transaction involving the sale of a home in Concord, CA. As a Realtor who regularly works with probate attorneys to help families sell homes through the probate process, I can confidently say that Lauren is truly top notch.

Lauren was proactive from the beginning, making sure everything was organized and that I had what I needed to handle the real estate side of the transaction smoothly. Her communication was excellent, she responded quickly, stayed ahead of deadlines, and ensured that all required items were handled in advance, which made the entire process feel seamless for everyone involved.

Probate transactions can often be complex, but Lauren’s professionalism, attention to detail, and client-focused approach made this one feel easy and well-managed from start to finish.

I highly recommend Lauren Rios to anyone in the San Francisco Bay Area looking for a knowledgeable, responsive, and trustworthy probate or trust attorney. She is a true professional and a great partner to work with.

I highly recommend the services of Lauren Rios. I restated my Living Trust with Lauren five years after drafting the original, and I found her to be both meticulous and patient in ensuring all my assets were properly incorporated. She was always available to answer my questions and provided thoughtful guidance throughout the process.

One instance that stood out was when Lauren carefully reviewed the beneficiary designations on my non-retirement accounts. At first, I was surprised by her request for details, but I soon realized I had overlooked adding the Trust as a beneficiary to a bank CD I had opened after the original Living Trust was created. Her thorough approach not only caught this oversight but also reinforced the importance of maintaining consistency in estate planning. Throughout the process, Lauren remained patient and understanding, never making me feel judged for the lapse but instead guiding me toward the necessary corrections with clarity and professionalism.

She was also highly conscientious, going the extra mile to make photocopies of my Advance Health Care Directive and HIPAA Authorizations so I could easily provide them to my medical providers. Since I was scheduled to visit my primary care provider, delivering these documents was seamless, and they are now properly scanned into my medical records.

Looking back, I am deeply grateful for the decision to restate my Living Trust with Lauren. There were several aspects of estate planning I had not considered at the time, and thanks to her diligence, I now feel confident that my executor will be able to carry out my wishes effectively when the time comes.

If I could give 1,000 stars, I would. I am so happy and satisfied with the attention they have given us since the day they decided to take my mom's case. The road wasn't short, but it was worth the wait, thank God. They managed to get my mom out of immigration detention at the Mesa Verde Detention Center in Bakersfield in February 2019, after my mom had turned herself in to immigration at the border crossing between Tijuana and San Ysidro, California, in October 2017. They didn't grant her parole because, "according to" the judge, she was a risk of not showing up in court again. Thank God these lawyers went to the detention center where my mom was being held to review immigration cases and they were interested in her case. I still remember that day; my mom called me crying with emotion, and that's where the fight to get her out of detention began. And although the lawyer, Tess Feldman, is no longer working at this legal center, I want to emphasize that I will always be there for her. I am grateful to Attorney Tess Feldman for all the support she gave us and for helping us secure bail for my mother so she could fight her asylum case outside of detention. When my mother was released, she already had a petition filed by my older sister, a U.S. citizen, and after more than five years, today, December 8, 2022, my mother received her permanent resident card!

I also want to thank Attorney Martha, my mother's current attorney, who helped us a lot. She always advised and guided my mother to achieve positive results, and I want to thank the entire team at Community Legal Services in East Palo Alto. I truly admire these people who helped my mother so much. We will be forever grateful. May God protect and bless them always.

I also want to tell those who haven't had a good experience with these lawyers that not all cases are the same. Personally, my mother's case seemed very difficult to me for certain reasons, but thanks to God and these lawyers, they did the impossible for my mother. Thank you again, thank you so much!

Don’t recommend. I had a really bad stressful experience. Priya changed her assistant many times, it was hard to reach out for questions because it was always someone new, and whenever I had questions to them they didn’t know anything about my case, or they would give me a snarky attitude about it. Many times they wouldn’t answer the phone either.
The process was very long supposedly because of Covid. I applied for a I-130 in Sept. 2020 and I got my green card this year. I needed to travel before December 2022 to get a new passport since my country doesn’t have an embassy in the US, but since I couldn’t leave the country, (I was pregnant during the process and I was scared they wouldn’t let me in again) I couldn’t renew my passport and now I might have to wait to visit my family in my country until I get my citizenship.
They might not be at fault 100%, but still they were definitely very slow so I feel like it could’ve been faster. Another thing was, that Inmigration had a problem with the birth certificate I sent, I remember asking Priya if I needed the long translated version of my birth certificate or the short version) They said the short translated birth certificate would be okay. Obviously it wasn’t, so I had to get my birth certificate translated AGAIN! Which is pretty expensive. And when I had my interview, they asked why my lawyer wasn’t there, that they were supposed to be there and I needed to sign a letter saying that I was by myself.
